subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data are organized in clear, labelled tables; graphs include axes, trendlines and RÂ² values.
Instrumental uncertainties are quoted and propagated, and error bars are shown on the graph.
weaknesses: Uncertainty treatment is inconsistent (same Â±0.003 assigned without justification, propagation transcription errors).
Dataâprocessing choices (linear fit despite a plateau) introduce conceptual flaws.
Minor typographical slips in units and table headers reduce precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication of data and processing is clear but not precise, and the consideration of uncertainties has significant omissions. Although some processing is accurate, the flawed uncertainty propagation and mismatched modelling limit this criterion to the lower end of the 3â4 band. To reflect these gaps, a score of 2 is assigned.

strengths: The research question is sharply focused and placed in a wellâdefined, realâworld context (professional tournaments, temperature ranges, COR relevance).
The methodology is described stepâbyâstep with diagrams, photographs, a controlledâvariable table and trial numbers, allowing reproducibility.
Methodological considerations are thoroughly explained, linking equilibrium time, ball selection and uncertainty propagation to data sufficiency.
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies in unit notation (Celsius vs. Kelvin) and subscript labeling reduce precision.
Small ambiguities remain (e.g., camera frameârate details, continuous waterâbath monitoring).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of Research Design sit firmly in the 5â6 band: specific context, reproducible methodology and wellâexplained methodological considerations. Minor slips in unit conversions and a few ambiguities prevent a perfect 6, so a score of 5 reflects the high quality and completeness of the design while leaving room for those small improvements.

strengths: The conclusion restates numerical findings, rejects the null hypothesis and ties interpretations directly to the analysed data.
Comparisons with literature (Cross 2014) and ITF specifications effectively situate results within the accepted scientific context.
weaknesses: The conclusion overstates linearity and does not address the observed plateau at higher temperatures.
Inconsistent reporting of uncertainty values reduces consistency.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the conclusion is relevant and supported by analysis and situates findings in context, it overlooks key data features (the plateau) and contains minor inconsistencies. This performance aligns with the middle of the 3â4 band; a score of 3 reflects solid achievement with room for deeper critical discussion.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified and their relative impacts (e.g., Â±0.3 % height variation, unknown core temperature) are explained.
Suggested improvements are realistic and relevant (longer equilibration, mechanical release, power analysis, new balls).
weaknesses: Improvements lack detailed explanation of feasibility and quantitative impact on uncertainty reduction.
Some suggestions (ball degradation protocol) would benefit from more procedural detail.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation clearly explains the relative impact of key methodological weaknesses, placing it in the top band for that strand, while improvements are realistic and wellâchosen but only described rather than fully explained, fitting the 3â4 band. A score of 4 balances these two performances within the criterion.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student states a sharply focused question and embeds it in a clearly delineated real-world context (professional tournaments, observed temperature range, COR relevance). Specific numerical ranges and sporting examples demonstrate that the context is both specific and appropriate, satisfying the descriptors for the top band, though a minor loss of precision in the wording (Â°C symbols, typographic slips) keeps the strand to the lower end of 5-6.

The student quotes instrumental uncertainties, propagates them with a standard formula and shows error bars on the graph. However, the same Â±0.003 COR uncertainty is assigned to every point without justification, and the propagation example contains transcription errors. These omissions indicate partial but not fully appropriate treatment, fitting the middle of the 3-4 band (3 marks).

Methodological considerations (equilibrium time, new balls, surface choice, release errors, propagation of uncertainties) are explained with clear links to data sufficiency and relevance. The student justifies 10 trials, 8 temperatures and controlled variables, moving beyond mere description. Occasional superficial statements prevent full sophistication, so 5 is appropriate.

Four specific weaknesses are identified and their quantitative or qualitative impacts (e.g., Â±0.3 % height variation, unknown core temperature) are discussed, explaining how each could skew COR. This explanation of relative impact aligns with the top band, though one weakness (sample size) is less developed, so 5 marks are awarded.

Step-by-step method, diagrams and photographs, controlled-variable table and trial numbers allow a competent reader to replicate the investigation. Most apparatus tolerances and positions are given and justified. Only minor ambiguities (exact camera frame rate, water-bath temperature monitoring details) remain, so the work meets the âallows reproductionâ requirement, warranting 5 marks.

Averages, COR calculations and a linear fit are executed correctly and matched to the graph; an ANOVA test is performed. Minor conceptual flaws (linear model despite non-linear trend, uncertainty propagation issues) introduce some inconsistency, but overall processing is largely accurate, placing the work solidly in the 3-4 band at 4.

Proposed improvements (longer equilibration, mechanical release, new balls, power analysis) are realistic and relevant, and most are briefly justified. The explanations, however, remain concise rather than fully elaborated on practicality and expected magnitude of benefit, so the strand meets the descriptor for âdescribedâ rather than âexplainedâ, earning 4 marks.

Raw and processed data are organised in labelled tables; units and uncertainties are mostly present. Graphs include axes, trendline and RÂ². Minor imprecisions (no unit in raw COR table header, typographic slips) limit absolute precision, therefore communication is clear but not impeccably precise, matching the 3-4 band and meriting 4.

The student compares results with literature gradients, ITF specifications and rubber-elasticity theory, citing sources. The comparison is relevant and partially analysed but not deeply critiqued, fulfilling the âsome relevant comparisonâ level. Thus 4 marks (upper end of 3-4 band) are appropriate.

The conclusion restates numerical findings, rejects the null hypothesis and links directly to analysed data and statistical tests, showing overall consistency. A slight over-statement of linearity and limited discussion of the plateau keep it from full alignment, so 4 marks within the 3-4 band are justified.

annotations.comment: The introduction effectively situates the study within professional tennis contexts, referencing specific tournament temperatures and real-world relevance.
The research question is precise, quantifying temperature points and uncertainties, and clearly defines measurement via video analysis.
The formula defining COR would benefit from explicit notation of units for rebound and initial heights to reinforce dimensional clarity.
The application of Gay-Lussacâs law omits the necessary conversion of Celsius to Kelvin, which is critical for correct pressureâtemperature relationships.
Subscript notation for the final pressure and temperature variables is inconsistent (Pâqâ, T); clarify and match subscripts throughout.
Including the expression $$E_p = mgh$$ provides a solid theoretical basis for the energy analysis of the falling tennis ball.
Consider adding an equation for elastic potential energy to complete the energyâtransformation framework.
The null and alternative hypotheses are clearly stated and linked to the theoretical discussion of gas laws and elasticity.
The prediction of a plateau at high temperatures would be more compelling with citation of specific rubber softening data or threshold values.
The temperature values listed in Table 1 ([5,10,â¦40]Â°C) differ from those in the research question (5,15,25,32,40Â°C), which may confuse replication.
The videoâanalysis software uncertainty is given in mÂ·sâ»Â¹, but COR depends on height in m; ensure units align with the measured quantity.
The controlledâvariable table quantifies potential impacts and control methods clearly, enhancing reproducibility.
The uncontrolled variables section acknowledges air currents and spin, but lacks quantitative estimation of residual effects.
The schematic setup is clear and wellâlabelled, which greatly aids in replicating the experimental arrangement.
Step 1 of the method should explicitly note verification of the 1 m drop height with a calibration measurement for accuracy.
To ensure precise ball temperature, consider continuous waterâbath monitoring using a probe rather than a oneâtime reading.
Raw data tables would be strengthened by including measurement uncertainties or standard deviations alongside average values.
The uncertainty propagation incorrectly uses ÎH = 0.5 m; it should apply the actual measurement uncertainty (e.g. Â±0.001 m) in the formula.
Graph 1 is wellâlabelled and includes a trend line with RÂ², facilitating clear interpretation of the data.
A linear fit is applied despite a clear plateau beyond 30 Â°C; consider a nonâlinear or piecewise model to better represent the trend.
Include the ANOVA test statistic (e.g. F-value) as well as the p-value to fully document the statistical analysis.
The conclusion asserts a positive linear relationship but does not address the observed plateau, overstating consistency.
The comparison with Cross (2014) and ITF specifications robustly situates findings within accepted scientific context.
The stated uncertainty at 20 Â°C (Â±0.004) differs from earlier Â±0.003; ensure consistent propagation results.
The evaluation identifies specific methodological weaknesses and explains their relative impacts, demonstrating critical insight.
The proposed improvements lack detailed explanation of feasibility and expected quantitative impact on uncertainty reduction.
The ball degradation section could be strengthened by describing a protocol for in situ pressure testing and material inspection.
Strengths are clearly articulated with links to reproducibility and statistical significance, reinforcing methodological rigor.
The discussion of strengths could include quantitative reproducibility metrics (e.g. coefficients of variation) to substantiate claims.
Bibliography entries show inconsistent formatting (dates, DOI inclusion); adopt a standard citation style (e.g. APA) throughout.

rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
rubricData.name: Physics IA
